name: Lonnie Plaxico
abstract: Lonnie Plaxico (born September 4, 1960) is an American jazz double bassist.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Plaxico plays bass on some tracks [of Ivey Divey]... Greg Osby, Gary Thomas, Mick Goodrick, Lonnie Plaxico and yourself... Audio-Visualscapes, another iteration… Actually the same group, Same personnel. Greg Osby, Gary Thomas, Mick Goodrick, Lonnie Plaxico and yourself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: it had Mick Goodrick on guitar, let’s see, Lonnie Plaxico on bass and Greg Osby on alto and Gary Thomas on tenor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Plaxico had been working with me and Lonnie couldn’t do the gig coming up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
